case.title: FORD v. ATTORNEY GENERAL OF PENNSYLVANIA

opinion.h: FORD v. ATTORNEY GENERAL OF PENNSYLVANIA, 364 U.S. 291 (1960)
FORD v. ATTORNEY GENERAL OF PENNSYLVANIA ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT FOR THE EASTERN DISTRICT OF PENNSYLVANIA. No. 255. Decided October 10, 1960.
opinion.p: Anne X. Alpern, Attorney General of Pennsylvania, and Frank P. Lawley, Jr., Deputy Attorney General, for appellees.
The motion to affirm is granted and the judgment is affirmed.
364 U.S. 291 VAUGHN v. OHIO. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF OHIO. No. 24, Misc. Decided October 10, 1960.
C. Watson Hover and Harry C. Schoettmer for appellee.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
